intro: Rhetinantha notylioglossa, also known as the "Golden Trumpet Vine," is a plant species native to the tropical forests of South America, characterized by its showy, trumpet-shaped flowers and woody vines. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. The bioactive constituents of Rhetinantha notylioglossa have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential health benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, Rhetinantha notylioglossa is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to make a decoction, or dried and used as a tincture, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
